# tslog

> Universal TypeScript logger for Node.js, browsers, Deno, Bun, and workers. Zero runtime dependencies. Pretty or JSON output, sub-loggers, secret/PII/prompt masking, async-context correlation, structured error/cause formatting. A strong fit for AI/agentic apps needing structured, redacted, correlated logs.

## Import
```ts
import { Logger, log } from "tslog"; // class + ready-to-use default instance
```

## Core API
- `new Logger({ type: "json" })` — structured one-object-per-line output (production / observability / LLM ingestion). Default `type` is env-aware: pretty in an interactive TTY, json in CI / non-TTY / when `NO_COLOR` is set.
- `log.info|warn|error|debug|trace|silly|fatal(...)` — fields-first or string-first: `log.info({ userId: 42 }, "ready")` or `log.info("ready", { userId: 42 })`.
- `log.getSubLogger({ name })` / `log.child({ name })` — child logger (aliases); inherits settings, names, prefixes, transports.
- `log.runInContext({ requestId }, fn)` — AsyncLocalStorage correlation: fields attach to every log inside `fn`, across awaits. `log.getContext()` reads them.
- `log.attachTransport(t)` extra sink; `log.use(mw)` middleware; `log.flush()` / `await using`.

## Settings are GROUPED (no flat keys)
- `type`, `name`, `minLevel: "INFO"` (name or 0..6).
- `mask: { keys: ["password","apiKey","token","prompt"], paths: ["user.password","*.token"], caseInsensitive, regex, censor }` — redact secrets/PII/prompts by key, dotted path, or regex. Regexes always apply globally (no `g` flag needed); keys/regex also mask inside `Map`/`Set` contents; shared/circular references resolve to the same masked clone.
- `json: { messageKey, levelKey, timeKey, errorKey }`, `pretty: { timeZone, style }`, `stack: { capture: "off" }`, `meta: { property, attachContext }`.

## Output shape (type:"json")
Flat, fields-first: `{ message, level, levelId, time, ...yourFields, error?, _meta: { v: 5, runtime, logLevelId, ... } }`.

## Presets (subpaths)
- `tslog/presets/pino` — `pinoTransport`/`pinoFormat` (pino NDJSON).
- `tslog/otel` — `otelFormat`/`toOtelRecord` (OpenTelemetry logs).
- `tslog/presets/genai` — `genai({ model, inputTokens, outputTokens, costUsd })` → spread into a log call.

## Transports (subpaths, opt-in)
- `tslog/transports/file` — `fileTransport({ path, format, append })` (Node; buffered, flush on dispose).
- `tslog/transports/http` — `httpTransport({ url, batchSize, flushIntervalMs })` (batched fetch).
- `tslog/transports/ringbuffer` — `ringBufferTransport({ size })` (in-memory; `.dump()`/`.clear()`).
- `tslog/transports/worker` — `workerTransport({ destination: "file"|"stdout"|"stderr", path, format })` (Node-only; runs sink I/O on a worker thread to keep it off the event loop — does NOT speed up `log.info()` itself).

## Notes for code generation
- Prefer `type: "json"` and `minLevel: "INFO"` for machine-read logs.
- Use `getSubLogger`/`child` per request/agent, `runInContext` for correlation.
- Always set `mask.keys`/`mask.paths` when logging objects that may hold secrets or prompts.

## Links
- Recipes: ./RECIPES.md
- Repo: https://github.com/fullstack-build/tslog
